headline: FGV Holdings Posts 8th Consecutive Quarterly Loss
context: Felda Global Ventures Holdings reported its 8th consecutive quarterly loss in Q4 2025, with accumulated losses of RM4.2 billion since its troubled IPO in 2012, the largest in Malaysian corporate history for a GLC.

cards.big: 8 straight quarterly losses — FGV has lost RM4.2 billion since IPO while 112,000 FELDA settlers who own shares watch value evaporate
cards.sub: IPO price of RM4.55 per share collapsed to RM0.82 — settler investments lost 82% of their value

cards.big: FGV's plantation yields at 16 tonnes per hectare are 28% below industry average of 22 tonnes
cards.sub: Operational inefficiency costs RM1.4 billion annually in lost production — management failure, not market conditions

cards.big: 112,000 settlers received 810 shares each at IPO — holdings now worth RM664 versus original RM3,685 per person
cards.sub: Settlers were told IPO would secure their financial future — instead it destroyed RM340 million of their collective wealth

cards.big: FGV board changed 4 times since 2018 — each new board blames predecessors while losses continue uninterrupted
cards.sub: 3 former FGV executives charged with corruption totalling RM380 million — cases still pending after 5 years

cards.big: FGV was supposed to turn FELDA into a global agribusiness champion — instead it turned settler savings into the biggest GLC losses in history
Should FGV be taken private at a fair premium to protect remaining settler value and restructure without market pressure?
